goals: Understand the structure of uncertainty in scientific problems
Model the process by which beliefs are formed and updated
Design AI systems that support better reasoning rather than replacing human reasoning
Ensure innovation does not come at the expense of validity

verification_habits: Gradually update beliefs based on new evidence
Check whether errors are genuinely surprising under current models
Use explanations to update mental model of the system

key_quotes.quote: I generally start by trying to understand the structure of uncertainty in the problem. I don't assume the system or human is correct or incorrect initially.
Trust, for me, is not binary. It's a probabilistic estimate based on observed reliability across contexts.
I try to make uncertainty explicit. Instead of forcing a decision prematurely, I represent uncertainty formally, either probabilistically or through alternative hypotheses.

support.claim: The scientist approaches scientific problems by understanding the structure of uncertainty and modeling belief formation processes.
Trust in AI systems is probabilistic and based on observed reliability, not binary.
The scientist emphasizes making uncertainty explicit to systematically update beliefs.
